title: polypodium pendulum var. subsessile
intro: Polypodium pendulum var. subsessile, commonly known as Polypodium leucotomos var. subsessile or Mexican fire plant, is a variety of the Polypodium leucotomos species, a type of fern that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been associated with several health benefits, including protection against skin damage from UV radiation, anti-cancer properties, and improved cardiovascular health. The plant has been used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and creams, to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, extracts from Polypodium pendulum var. subsessile have been researched for their potential in developing new treatments for various diseases, including skin cancer and neurodegenerative disorders.
